title:
Catalogue guide
	 - data.govt.nz
content:
- Using data.govt.nz APIs Technical documentation and code examples for developer use with the data.govt.nz Application Programming Interfaces (APIs). About data.govt.nz APIs The metadata and many datasets on data.govt.nz are available for use via an Application Programming Interface (API). When using our API you need to know what you can and cannot do with the data and where to find technical documentation. Do's and dont's of using the data.govt.nz API Data.govt.nz's data APIs are made openly available for users and it's worth taking a moment to find out what you can and cannot do when using our APIs in your own applications. Accessing dataset metadata via API Data.govt.nz's datasets, organisations and groups can be accessed and queried as JSON date through the metadata API; this page describes how. Accessing machine readable data via API The data.govt.nz DataStore API allows you to access and query the rows and columns of raw data listed by agencies in machine readable CSV format via a JSON API endpoint. How to export all datasets from data.govt.nz into a CSV Using the ckanapi-exporter tool you can extract all dataset metadata into a single CSV file. How to export an agency's datasets as a data package Export an agencies datasets into sub-folders including any hosted or referenced data files and a json file with metadata.

### Using data.govt.nz APIs
Technical documentation and code examples for developer use with the data.govt.nz Application Programming Interfaces (APIs).
- About data.govt.nz APIs The metadata and many datasets on data.govt.nz are available for use via an Application Programming Interface (API). When using our API you need to know what you can and cannot do with the data and where to find technical documentation.

#### About data.govt.nz APIs
The metadata and many datasets on data.govt.nz are available for use via an Application Programming Interface (API). When using our API you need to know what you can and cannot do with the data and where to find technical documentation.
- Do's and dont's of using the data.govt.nz API Data.govt.nz's data APIs are made openly available for users and it's worth taking a moment to find out what you can and cannot do when using our APIs in your own applications.

#### Do's and dont's of using the data.govt.nz API
Data.govt.nz's data APIs are made openly available for users and it's worth taking a moment to find out what you can and cannot do when using our APIs in your own applications.
- Accessing dataset metadata via API Data.govt.nz's datasets, organisations and groups can be accessed and queried as JSON date through the metadata API; this page describes how.

#### Accessing dataset metadata via API
Data.govt.nz's datasets, organisations and groups can be accessed and queried as JSON date through the metadata API; this page describes how.
- Accessing machine readable data via API The data.govt.nz DataStore API allows you to access and query the rows and columns of raw data listed by agencies in machine readable CSV format via a JSON API endpoint.

#### Accessing machine readable data via API
The data.govt.nz DataStore API allows you to access and query the rows and columns of raw data listed by agencies in machine readable CSV format via a JSON API endpoint.
- How to export all datasets from data.govt.nz into a CSV Using the ckanapi-exporter tool you can extract all dataset metadata into a single CSV file.

#### How to export all datasets from data.govt.nz into a CSV
Using the ckanapi-exporter tool you can extract all dataset metadata into a single CSV file.
- How to export an agency's datasets as a data package Export an agencies datasets into sub-folders including any hosted or referenced data files and a json file with metadata.

#### How to export an agency's datasets as a data package
Export an agencies datasets into sub-folders including any hosted or referenced data files and a json file with metadata.
- Releasing data on data.govt.nz Guidance and tutorials on using the data.govt.nz catalogue to publish open data listings. This includes a helpful checklist, general user help and specific 'how to' articles for New Zealand government agencies listing open datasets. Open data release checklist A draft checklist of the things you should consider before releasing open data. What is a publisher account and how do I apply? Government agencies can publish and update their datasets on the data.govt.nz catalogue by applying for a publisher account. Apply for a data.govt.nz publisher account Add government datasets to the listing on data.govt.nz. What metadata should I include with my dataset? Metadata describes your dataset to others in a standardised way. Having good quality metadata helps people discover and use your dataset. This guidance provides a description and examples of good practice metadata when releasing on data.govt.nz. Adding datasets to data.govt.nz Learn how to prepare a machine readable CSV file and publish to the data.govt.nz data catalogue. How do I add or update our Chief Executive expenses? Public Service Commission ask agencies to disclose their Departmental Secretary or Chief Executive Expenses at least annually. Data.govt.nz allows you to self-manage the update process to the CE Expenses datasets. This allows agencies to publish faster and meet the disclosure deadline. How to correctly enter your open licence in ArcGIS Online This walkthrough will help you correctly set up Creative Commons licences for your data layers in ArcGIS Online open data portal. Setting licences correctly here flows through to data.govt.nz when you make use of automated harvesting. How to respond to a data request Data.govt.nz allows people to request open data from agencies with a data request form. This guidance page helps agencies resolve data requests they receive by using the response features built into data.govt.nz. Automating dataset updates Data.govt.nz adopted an international data harvesting open standard (data.json) to automate agency dataset updates and additions. There are tools available to help generate the correct format and the open standard data schema is detailed with examples. Formats for open data Machine and human readable formats, such as PDF, CSV, and XLS, ensures that you maximise the value of your open data while complying with open government data and information policies. Qualities of a good open data API There are 15 key things you can do to ensure your data API is user-friendly. How do I set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)? Following best practice guidance from CERT NZ, we have made Publisher accounts on data.govt.nz more secure by using Multi-Factor Authentication.

### Releasing data on data.govt.nz
Guidance and tutorials on using the data.govt.nz catalogue to publish open data listings. This includes a helpful checklist, general user help and specific 'how to' articles for New Zealand government agencies listing open datasets.
- Open data release checklist A draft checklist of the things you should consider before releasing open data.

#### Open data release checklist
A draft checklist of the things you should consider before releasing open data.
- What is a publisher account and how do I apply? Government agencies can publish and update their datasets on the data.govt.nz catalogue by applying for a publisher account.

#### What is a publisher account and how do I apply?
Government agencies can publish and update their datasets on the data.govt.nz catalogue by applying for a publisher account.
- Apply for a data.govt.nz publisher account Add government datasets to the listing on data.govt.nz.

#### Apply for a data.govt.nz publisher account
Add government datasets to the listing on data.govt.nz.
- What metadata should I include with my dataset? Metadata describes your dataset to others in a standardised way. Having good quality metadata helps people discover and use your dataset. This guidance provides a description and examples of good practice metadata when releasing on data.govt.nz.

#### What metadata should I include with my dataset?
Metadata describes your dataset to others in a standardised way. Having good quality metadata helps people discover and use your dataset. This guidance provides a description and examples of good practice metadata when releasing on data.govt.nz.
- Adding datasets to data.govt.nz Learn how to prepare a machine readable CSV file and publish to the data.govt.nz data catalogue.

#### Adding datasets to data.govt.nz
Learn how to prepare a machine readable CSV file and publish to the data.govt.nz data catalogue.
- How do I add or update our Chief Executive expenses? Public Service Commission ask agencies to disclose their Departmental Secretary or Chief Executive Expenses at least annually. Data.govt.nz allows you to self-manage the update process to the CE Expenses datasets. This allows agencies to publish faster and meet the disclosure deadline.

#### How do I add or update our Chief Executive expenses?
Public Service Commission ask agencies to disclose their Departmental Secretary or Chief Executive Expenses at least annually. Data.govt.nz allows you to self-manage the update process to the CE Expenses datasets. This allows agencies to publish faster and meet the disclosure deadline.
- How to correctly enter your open licence in ArcGIS Online This walkthrough will help you correctly set up Creative Commons licences for your data layers in ArcGIS Online open data portal. Setting licences correctly here flows through to data.govt.nz when you make use of automated harvesting.

#### How to correctly enter your open licence in ArcGIS Online
This walkthrough will help you correctly set up Creative Commons licences for your data layers in ArcGIS Online open data portal. Setting licences correctly here flows through to data.govt.nz when you make use of automated harvesting.
- How to respond to a data request Data.govt.nz allows people to request open data from agencies with a data request form. This guidance page helps agencies resolve data requests they receive by using the response features built into data.govt.nz.

#### How to respond to a data request
Data.govt.nz allows people to request open data from agencies with a data request form. This guidance page helps agencies resolve data requests they receive by using the response features built into data.govt.nz.
- Automating dataset updates Data.govt.nz adopted an international data harvesting open standard (data.json) to automate agency dataset updates and additions. There are tools available to help generate the correct format and the open standard data schema is detailed with examples.

#### Automating dataset updates
Data.govt.nz adopted an international data harvesting open standard (data.json) to automate agency dataset updates and additions. There are tools available to help generate the correct format and the open standard data schema is detailed with examples.
- Formats for open data Machine and human readable formats, such as PDF, CSV, and XLS, ensures that you maximise the value of your open data while complying with open government data and information policies.

#### Formats for open data
Machine and human readable formats, such as PDF, CSV, and XLS, ensures that you maximise the value of your open data while complying with open government data and information policies.
- Qualities of a good open data API There are 15 key things you can do to ensure your data API is user-friendly.

#### Qualities of a good open data API
There are 15 key things you can do to ensure your data API is user-friendly.
- How do I set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)? Following best practice guidance from CERT NZ, we have made Publisher accounts on data.govt.nz more secure by using Multi-Factor Authentication.

#### How do I set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
Following best practice guidance from CERT NZ, we have made Publisher accounts on data.govt.nz more secure by using Multi-Factor Authentication.
- Step by step guides Step by step guides to help you work through processes involved with managing and releasing data. Releasing open data on data.govt.nz Guide to reviewing and safely releasing open data for reuse.
Step by step guides to help you work through processes involved with managing and releasing data.
- Releasing open data on data.govt.nz Guide to reviewing and safely releasing open data for reuse.

#### Releasing open data on data.govt.nz
Guide to reviewing and safely releasing open data for reuse.
- Showcase Read inspiring New Zealand stories about innovative projects that use open data. You can also hear about the challenges, successes and benefits of initiatives to publish open data. Serendipity: the hidden value of open data Te Papa has used Wellington City Council's freely available cyber twin of Wellington City so museum visitors can explore the impact of tsunamis. Homes.co.nz - Helping Kiwis make smart property decisions HERE Maps - The changing face of automotive navigation Open source licence guidance for the New Zealand Government through public participation Thundermaps - Staying safe from hazards around you Dumpark - Making data and information accessible DigitalNZ - Making it easy to find New Zealand’s digital treasures Kamo Place Race - Town planning made lean and agile LAWA - Connecting with New Zealand’s environment through data Nest Finder - Accessing essential travel information while on the road State of the Nation - Telling a different story about the nation ANZ Truckometer Campermate Industry Benchmarking Tool MarineMate app Mogeo School Finder NZ tides prediction data Charities Register In 2011 Charities Services (then the Charities Commission) made advanced search functionality available for the Charities Register and licensed the data for re-use under a Creative Commons BY licence. GeoNet InfoConnect (Decommissioned on 1 October 2024) Land Cover Database LINZ Data Service Local councils The Department of Internal Affairs manages the local councils website at localcouncils.govt.nz, which presents data from New Zealand’s 78 local authorities in a consistent format that enables it to be compared and analysed. New Zealand Legislation website PositioNZ GNSS Data 2006 Census Economic indicators Infoshare Official statistics Environment Canterbury Public Transport Open Data Wellington City Council Geospatial Open Data HuntingSpots Affordability in NZ Affordability in NZ is an open source web application, created by transportation consultancy MRCagney, which uses open government data to show people which suburbs will be most affordable for them. Craft Beer Industry Insights The ANZ Bank uses open data to create industry insight reports that inform investment in the craft beer industry. Open source software release saves nearly $1m
Read inspiring New Zealand stories about innovative projects that use open data. You can also hear about the challenges, successes and benefits of initiatives to publish open data.
